properties.remark: TREE DAMAGE NEAR INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 16 AND HIGHWAY 49. TREE LIMBS OF UNKNOWN SIZE.

ON FEBRUARY 2, 2019 THERE WAS A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AT THE SACRAMENTO RIVER NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE NORTH OF COLUSA. A WEATHER SPOTTER TOOK A VIDEO OF THE TOUCHDOWN, WHICH OCCURRED IN A WETLANDS AREA. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED.
HALF DOLLAR SIZED HAIL REPORTED NORTH OF COLUSA.
TREE AND FENCE DAMAGE NEAR CARLSON ROAD AND HUTCHINSON ROAD IN YUBA CITY.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT APPROXIMATELY 5:50 PM PST ON SATURDAY, FEBRUARY 2ND, JUST SOUTH OF YUBA CITY, CA. THIS TORNADO TOUCH DOWN WAS VERY BRIEF AS IT TRACKED TO THE NORTH NORTHWEST. RADAR DATA AND THE DAMAGE PATTERN WERE USED TO CONFIRM THE TOUCHDOWN. SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN AND THERE WAS MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME. LEAVES AND BRANCHES WERE COMPLETELY STRIPPED OFF ONE SIDE OF A TREE.
OVER THE PAST 2-3 HOURS 1.5 TO 2.5 INCHES OF RAIN HAVE FALLEN. A MUDSLIDE WAS REPORTED AT THIS PARTICULAR LOCATION WITH THE ROAD BEING CLOSED. THE CLOSER GOES FROM WOODHOUSE MINE TO WEST POINT.
CALTRANS IS REPORTING THAT THE ROADWAY IS COMPLETELY FLOODED.
MINOR MUD AND ROCK SLIDE WITHIN THE CAMP WILDFIRE AT SKYWAY RD AND DOGTOWN RD. SOFTBALL SIZE ROCKS ON THE ROAD.
MUD/DIRT/ROCK COVERING MOST OF THE ROADWAY AT KELSEY CREEK DR AND STRAHELI DRIVE.
MUD/DIRT/ROCK BLOCKING ONE LANE HIGHWAY 70 NEAR THE NEW BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT.
ROCK AND MUD SLIDE KELSEY CREEK DR 2 1/2 MILES PASSED WIGHT WAY.
TUOLUMNE COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTING FLASH FLOODING AT WOODS CREEK NEAR STOCKTON ST IN SONORA, CA.
ROADS FLOODED AROUND ANRON LN AND OLD ALTURAS ROAD. SMALL HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.

AS OF 6:35 PM WE ARE STILL GETTING REPORTS OF SEVERE FLOODING WITH FLOODED ROADS AROUND ANRON LN AND OLD ALTURAS ROAD. CLOUGH CREEK NORMALLY 5 FEET WIDE IS 100-200 FEET WIDE. WATER IS CREEPING TOWARD HOUSES. 3 INCHES OF HAIL DEPTH, LARGEST SIZE NICKLE.
CLOUGH CREEK WATER LEVELS ARE GETTING CLOSE TO OVER FLOWING HIGHWAY 299. HIGHWAY 299 IS SEEING FLOODING AND IS BARELY PASSABLE IN THE WARNED AREA.
MANY ROADS ARE FLOODED OUT IN BELL VISTA. CREEKS WITHIN THE FLASH FLOOD WARNING ARE RISING RAPIDLY.

8 INCHES OF WATER OVER DRY CREEK AND DESCHUTES ROADS IN BELLA VISTA. WATER OVER THE SMALL BRIDGE BY THE POST OFFICE. THE FIRE STATION HAD FLOODED OUT AND 1-2 INCHES OF WATER IS FLOWING THROUGH THE STATION.
TREE DAMAGE OR DOWNED TREES REPORTED NEAR WEST SACRAMENTO AVE AND RIVER ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED.
FLOODING ACROSS ALL THREE LANES SOUTHBOUND ON HIGHWAY 99.
ROCK AND MUD FLOWS OVER ROADS NEAR WHISKEYTOWN DAM. THIS IS WITHIN THE CARR BURN SCAR AREA. REPORTED TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

HEAVY RAINFALL AROUND 9PM FRIDAY CAUSED FLOODING NEAR ROCK CREEK AND PAIGE BOULDER CREEK NEAR THE COMMUNITY OF OLD SHASTA. ROCK CREEK JUMPED ITS BANKS AND OCCUPIED PORTIONS OF THE FLOODPLAIN ALONG ROCK CREEK ROAD AND OVER TOPPED SEVERAL CROSSINGS THAT GOT PLUGGED BY WOODY DEBRIS. EXTENSIVE SCOUR OCCURRED ALONG BOTH CREEKS.

NUMEROUS REPORTS OF ROADWAY FLOODING IN STOCKTON DUE TO HEAVY RAIN AND ACCUMULATING HAIL ON MAY 19TH. LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED MARCH LANE AND QUAIL LAKES BEING SHUT DOWN AND HAMMER LANE BETWEEN LORRAINE AVE AND GIRARDI WAY DUE TO FLOODING.
1 1/2 FOOT OF WATER IN WEST BOUND LANE.
STATE ROUTE 174 ON PLACER COUNTY SIDE OF BORDER JUST EAST OF BEAR RIVER RD. ROADWAY FLOODING.
FLOODING OF ENTIRE WEST BOUND OFF RAMP BOTH LANES IN COLFAX.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN SHASTA LAKE CITY. PICTURE SENT VIA TWITTER.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN REDDING.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF GOLF BALL TO TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED ON THE EAST SIDE OF REDDING FROM 8:05 PM TO 8:20 PM. PICTURES SENT TO US VIA FACEBOOK.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED NEAR ENTERPRISE HIGH SCHOOL IN REDDING.

PING PONG SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED NEAR HARTNELL AVE AND VICTOR AVE IN REDDING, CA. PICTURE ON TWITTER.
MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED AT HIGHWAY 299 AND CRYSTAL CREEK RD AND HIGHWAY 299 AND TRINITY MOUNTAIN RD BY NATIONAL PARK SERVICE.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED AT TUNNEL MILLS CAMPGROUND AROUND 5:51 PM. PICTURE SENT VIA FACEBOOK.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED NEAR ALTA, CA AROUND 6:55 PM. PICTURE SENT VIA FACEBOOK.
ONE INCH HAIL WAS OBSERVED 7 MILES NORTHWEST OF RED BLUFF ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 9TH. HAIL FELL FOR ABOUT 5 TO 10 MINUTES. MINOR DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO TREES AND PLANTS.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES.
ELECTRICAL POLE DOWN AND ARCING.
LARGE TREE BRANCH BLOCKING BOTH LANES.
LARGE TREE BRANCHES (SIZE OF CAR) DOWN ACROSS EASTBOUND LANE OF SR 299.
LARGE OAK TREE BRANCH DOWN BLOCKING ENTIRE ROADWAY.
HAIL UP TO 1.5 INCH DIAMETER ON NORTH SIDE OF BLACK BUTTE LAKE NEAR BUCK HORN CAMPGROUND. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS AND ROCK SLIDES BLOCKED HIGHWAY 70 ON WEDNESDAY FROM 7 PM PDT TO MIDNIGHT FROM JARBO GAP TO GREENVILLE WYE (JUNCTION OF SR 70 AND SR 89). ROAD CULVERTS WERE ALSO BLOCKED WITH DEBRIS, REQUIRING LANE CLOSURES FOR CLEANUP TODAY. DEBRIS WAS ASSOCIATED WITH CAMP FIRE BURNED AREA.
RECEIVED A REPORT FROM THE LOCAL MEDIA THAT A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SAW SEVERAL TREES AND/OR LARGE LIMBS IN THE ROADWAY ON WASHINGTON ROAD OFF HWY 20.
SPOTTER SO47 LOCATED ON HIGHWAY 113 NORTH OF DAVIS.
A TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY A UC DAVIS ATMOSPHERIC SCIENCE STUDENT TO THE EAST OF HIGHWAY 101A AND HIGHWAY 29 AROUND 6:41 PM. NO KNOWN DAMAGE AT THIS TIME.
DOWN POWER LINES AND TREES DUE TO WIND GUSTS
THE COTTAGE MESONET STATION, AT AN ELEVATION OF 6064 FEET, REPORTED A NON-THUNDERSTORM RELATED WIND GUST OF 64 MPH. STRONG WINDS LASTED THROUGH THE LATE EVENING.
THE BLUE MOUNTAIN LOOKOUT MESONET STATION, AT AN ELEVATION OF 6043 FEET, REPORTED A NON-THUNDERSTORM RELATED WIND GUST OF 56 MPH.
